exemplar.title: Did the Detroit Lions have a statistical "home field advantage," as defined by both win-loss ratios and point differentials, in the 2022 and 2023 seasons?

detailedExemplarData.title: Did the Detroit Lions have a statistical "home field advantage," as defined by both win-loss ratios and point differentials, in the 2022 and 2023 seasons?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear structure with distinct headings (Introduction, Methodology, Evaluation, Conclusion)
Coherent logical development following research question, plan, analysis, and conclusion
weaknesses: Redundant narrative and repeated explanations interrupt flow
Verbosity in anecdotes and mid-paragraph references reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is well organized and coherent (band 3) with clear sectional flow, but unnecessary digressions and occasional repetition prevent it from being exceptionally seamless (conciseness at band 2). Overall, a score of 3 reflects strong structure and logic tempered by verbosity.

strengths: Consistent use of correct notation for hypotheses and expected-value formula
Clear, logical explanations of calculator procedures and p-value interpretations
Comprehensive tables of raw data and summary statistics aid reproducibility
weaknesses: No graphical or visual representations beyond tables
Minor inconsistencies in notation (e.g., mixing ÏÂ² symbols) and table formatting
Confusion in description between goodness-of-fit and independence tests
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and clarity are generally good (band 3), but representation is limited to tables (band 2) and small inconsistencies remain. A holistic score of 3 balances these factors.

strengths: Investigation driven by genuine personal interest in the team, showing independent thinking
Original data collection and choice of statistical tests demonstrate initiative
Personal voice and fan perspective sustain the narrative throughout
weaknesses: Proposed calculus extension is underdeveloped without a clear model
Creativity and approach are solid but not outstanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand sits in band 2âsignificant independent thinking and personal approachâbut does not reach the depth or originality required for band 3âso a score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Reflections link statistical results to understanding of home-field advantage
Acknowledges confounding factors and methodological limitations
Meaningful discussion of p-values and test outcomes
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive rather than deeply critical
Omits discussion of effect size and justification for pooling data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection demonstrates meaningful insight and some critique (band 2) but lacks the depth, critical analysis, and strong connections to understanding needed for band 3, meriting a 2.

strengths: Accurate execution of chi-squared and t-tests with correct p-value calculations
Sound interpretation of statistical significance and assumptions
Demonstrates thorough course-level understanding and effective application
weaknesses: Minor labeling errors in the contingency table
No explicit normality check or Î±-level specification
Calculus extension is noted but not fully substantiated mathematically
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is correct, detailed, and effectively applied (between bands 4 and 6). Minor errors and an underdeveloped extension keep it just below full sophistication, so a holistic score of 5 best reflects its strength.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personally meaningful NFL team, assembling two seasons of data, and deciding on appropriate statistical tests show significant evidence of independent thinking.  The mathematics used is routine, so the strand does not reach the âoutstandingâ level, but independence is clearly demonstrated.

The student explains the logic of both statistical tests, states assumptions, interprets results, and discusses limitations, evidencing good understanding throughout.  The level of insight is solid but not rigorous or original enough to merit higher bands, so band 4 is most fitting.

Calculated expected frequencies, means, and p-values are internally consistent and plausible.  Minor inconsistencies in the typed contingency table (e.g. mis-labelled losses) do not propagate into the numerical calculations.  Thus, mathematics is âcorrect with minor errorsâ.

Most of the writing relates directly to the statistical investigation, but there is noticeable verbosity: long digressions on Lions history, quotations about fandom, and generic reflections on sport.  These passages do not advance the mathematical argument and reduce conciseness.  Hence the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â matching band 2.

Symbols such as $\chi^2$, $E_{cell}$, $H_0$, $\mu_H$ and proper use of subscripts are employed and definitions of statistical terminology are given.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g. mixing $\Box^2$ and $\square^2$, occasional boldface digits in tables) prevent full consistency, but overall language and notation are appropriate through most of the report.

Explanations of calculator procedures, hypotheses, and interpretation of p-values are generally clear and logically sequenced, enabling the reader to replicate the work.  Minor typographical glitches and occasional redundancy keep the strand from full consistency, but clarity is achieved for most of the document.

A chi-squared test for independence and a 2-sample t-test are both at the standard HL AI course level and are applied correctly to a genuine data set.  The mathematics is relevant and at an appropriate level, though it is not used with particular sophistication, positioning the work in band 3.

The student provides extensive tables of raw data and calculated totals.  Apart from these, there are no other forms of representation such as graphs or diagrams that could have enriched the communication.  Therefore, the work shows âsome appropriate mathematical representationsâ without reaching the effectiveness expected for band 3.

The student reflects on the meaning of each p-value, the contradiction between tests, and the limitations posed by uneven samples.  Reflection is present throughout but remains largely descriptive rather than deeply critical, fitting the âmeaningful reflectionâ level.

Selecting relevant statistical tools, compiling original data tables, and reflecting on practical factors (uneven scheduling, other variables) display noticeable initiative.  Creativity is moderate rather than extraordinary, aligning with âsignificant creativity and initiativeâ.

The exploration follows a recognisable argumentative thread: the student introduces the question, outlines a plan, executes two statistical tests, then evaluates and concludes.  Transitions between sections are generally smooth and the reader can follow the logic without confusion.  Some digressions into fandom and anecdote interrupt the flow, preventing the level of âexcellentâ development expected for the top band, but the core storyline remains coherent and clearly developed.

The discussion acknowledges potential confounding variables and explains why certain tests were chosen, offering some critique of methodology.  The analysis stops short of deep statistical scrutiny (e.g. test assumptions, effect sizes), so the work demonstrates âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ.

Reflections explicitly link the statistical findings to the studentâs understanding of home-field advantage and broader sports contexts.  These links are clear but not extensively developed, fulfilling the descriptor for a clearâthough not strongâconnection to understanding.

Headings (Introduction, Plan of Action, Methodology, Evaluation, Conclusion) provide a clear structure and the tables are placed logically within the body.  Each section performs an identifiable purpose, giving the piece sound overall organisation.  However, the narrative sometimes doubles back (e.g. re-explaining the chi-squared test in two separate paragraphs) and references are occasionally inserted mid-paragraph, so the flow is not entirely seamless, keeping it in the âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionally well organisedâ category.

The narrative is driven by the studentâs own interest and experiences as a Lions fan, motivating each stage of the investigation.  This personal voice is sustained throughout and shapes the direction of the work, satisfying the descriptor for a âclear personal approachâ (band 2).

annotations.comment: Notation \square^2 is used inconsistently with earlier $$\chi^2$$. Replace \square^2 with the standard $$\chi^2$$ symbol throughout for clarity.
Detailed TI-84 instructions are clear but distract from mathematical reasoning. Summarize steps and focus commentary on interpretation.
The summary table of total wins and losses is correctly calculated and concisely presented, aiding the setup for the chi-squared test.
Reorganizing point differentials into home vs away lists is well-justified and aids calculator input, strengthening the methodological clarity.
The title is concise and clearly states the focus of the investigation, effectively setting reader expectations and signalling the mathematical context.
Administrative details like page count do not contribute to the mathematical content. These should be removed or placed in a separate cover sheet to maintain conciseness.
The introduction provides strong personal context but digresses into lengthy anecdote. Focus more tightly on research question to improve relevance and flow.
Consider trimming fan-history details that do not directly inform the statistical methodology. This will enhance conciseness and keep the narrative tightly focused.
Definition of home field advantage is clear but omits specification of the significance level (e.g., \(\alpha=0.05\)). Stating this here would strengthen the operational definition.
The description of the chi-squared test mixes goodness-of-fit and independence tests. Clarify that you are performing a test of independence, not a goodness-of-fit.
The plan for the 2-sample T-test omits discussion of normality assumptions. You should comment on checking distribution shapes or justifying the use of the test.
Figure 1 presents an extensive raw data table, but its density hinders readability. Consider splitting it by season or highlighting key columns for clarity.
The totals in the observed contingency matrix (13 wins, 6 losses) do not match some entries in Figure 1. Verify your winâloss counts and correct any mislabels.
The formula $$E_{cell}=\frac{(\text{row total})(\text{column total})}{(\text{grand total})}$$ is correctly stated and facilitates reproducibility of expected value calculations.
The expected-value table mixes decimal precision (e.g. 7.1890 vs 11.189). Use consistent rounding to avoid perceived inconsistency.
The explanation of p-value interpretation is clear and shows good understanding of statistical significance criteria.
Combining both seasons in one 2-sample T-test table obscures seasonal trends. Present separate season summaries before merging for clarity.
The TI-84 step list for the T-test is thorough but excessive. A concise outline of key calculator inputs would better balance conciseness and reproducibility.
The evaluation acknowledges many confounding factors and limitations, showing meaningful critical reflection on the studyâs scope.
The rationale for combining two seasons to isolate home-away effects is stated but lacks statistical justification; explain why pooling is valid despite roster changes.
Introducing a calculus-based approach to stadium design is a creative extension that demonstrates personal initiative and connects mathematics to real-world applications.
The combined home/away table uses mixed fonts and symbolic annotations (e.g. \mathfrak, bold numbers) that confuse interpretation. Standardize to plain numerals.
Hypotheses $$H_0:\mu_H=\mu_A$$ and $$H_1:\mu_H>\mu_A$$ are clearly stated, correctly aligning with the one-tailed 2-sample test.
Means $$\overline x_H\approx7.895$$ and $$\overline x_A\approx-2.\overline{88}$$ are calculated, but the notation for repeating decimals could confuse readers; clarify or round.
The extension mentions applying calculus but does not outline a specific mathematical model or steps. Providing a brief framework would strengthen the proposal.
The conclusion effectively contrasts the two test outcomes but omits a discussion of effect size or practical significance. Adding this would deepen the analysis.
Bibliographic entries vary in format and punctuation. Adopting a single citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) throughout will improve presentation and consistency.
